title: hordeum vulgare subsp. tetrastichum
intro: Hordeum vulgare subsp. tetrastichum is a subspecies of the common barley plant, a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family, characterized by its six-awned spikelets. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Hordeum vulgare subsp. tetrastichum include relieving symptoms of coughs and colds, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and other medicinal products.
